17.reason: [Error: This branch handles both pop (a==0) and end (-1) operations. When a==-1, it incorrectly prints the top element instead of terminating the loop. Also, when a==0 and i>0, it prints the element but does not decrement i before printing, causing the wrong element to be printed.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: This branch handles both pop (a==0) and end (-1) operations. When a==-1, it incorrectly prints the top element instead of terminating the loop. Also, when a==0 and i>0, it prints the element but does not decrement i before printing, causing the wrong element to be printed.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: This branch handles both pop (a==0) and end (-1) operations. When a==-1, it incorrectly prints the top element and decrements i, instead of terminating the loop. The condition should check for a==0 specifically before popping.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.25 , reason:  Accesses s[i-1] when i=0, causing out-of-bounds read. Also outputs for invalid operation -1.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  Missing check for stack overflow (capacity 100)
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.25 , reason:  No check for stack overflow; if i exceeds 99, it will write out of bounds and not output error.

16.reason: Incorrectly handles -1 as pop; should break when a==-1

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  Incorrectly handles -1 as pop; should break when a==-1
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.25 , reason:  The else clause handles a==-1 as a pop, but it should break the loop when a==-1. Also, it will try to access s[i-1] when i==0 (if -1 encountered after empty stack), causing out-of-bounds access.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  This block originally handled both 0 (pop) and -1 (end). Now it only handles 0.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.5 , reason:  This else block handles pop operations (when a==0 and stack is not empty) but also incorrectly handles -1 if the previous check is missing. With the fix above, this handles valid pops.

10.reason: Missing termination condition for input -1; the loop will continue to process -1 as an operation, causing erroneous pop.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.25 , reason:  Missing termination condition for input -1; the loop will continue to process -1 as an operation, causing erroneous pop.

13.reason: The original code treats -1 as a pop operation because it falls into the final else block. It should break the loop when -1 is encountered.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  The original code treats -1 as a pop operation because it falls into the final else block. It should break the loop when -1 is encountered.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.5 , reason:  The original code treats -1 as a pop operation because it falls into the final else block. It should break the loop or be ignored as per problem description (-1 represents end of operations).
